label: Bajas Militares Rusas
contestNote: Russia has never released official casualty figures. Ukrainian MoD reported ~1,285,700 total personnel losses by Mar 20, 2026. Kremlin's own classified assessments reportedly put KIA+WIA at 1,315,000 per Ukrainian HUR. CSIS Jan 2026: ~1.2M total.
deltaNote: Western estimate since Nov 2025; Telegraph reported Russia losing more soldiers than it can recruit

label: Bajas Militares Ucranianas
contestNote: Zelenskyy stated 55,000 killed (2026); UALosses project documented 86,142 by name + 89,000+ MIA. CSIS estimates 100K–140K killed. Western officials (Feb 2026): 250K–300K total KIA+WIA.

label: Muertes Civiles Verificadas (ONU)
contestNote: UN emphasizes real figures are considerably higher due to underreporting. 2025 was deadliest year for civilians since 2022; 31% rise over 2024.

label: Territorio Ucraniano Bajo Control Ruso
contestNote: Russia annexed Donetsk, Luhansk, Zaporizhzhia, and Kherson oblasts in Sep 2022, but doesn't fully control all four. Heavy fighting ongoing in Pokrovsk, Kostiantynivka, Lyman sectors.
deltaNote: Slow Russian gains despite massive losses; spring offensive pressure intensifying

label: Ucranianos Desplazados
deltaNote: Plus 3.7M internally displaced; 10.8M in need of humanitarian assistance in 2026

label: Activos Estatales Rusos Congelados
contestNote: Primarily held in Euroclear, Belgium (~€210B). G7 using interest (~$3B/year) to back $50B Ukraine loan. EU maintains freeze; US Treasury issued 30-day oil waiver in Mar 2026.

label: Días de Invasión a Gran Escala
Ayuda Occidental Total a Ucrania
contestNote: Includes US, EU, Germany, UK, Canada and other allies. US-Ukraine minerals deal signed Apr 2025 counts future US military aid as fund contributions. NATO targeting additional $15B in 2026.
deltaNote: Largest single US package; minerals deal signed Apr 30, 2025
